hydrodynamics.title: Interactive 3D Hydrodynamics Visualizer
hydrodynamics.intro:
We developed a web-based, real-time 3D visualization tool for massive unstructured mesh hydrodynamic simulations. It renders dynamic water surface elevations and flow fields directly in the browser.

The viewer features academic-grade colormaps for depth variation and interactive camera controls, built on top of WebGL and Three.js technologies. This aids in rapid data interpretation for environmental engineering projects.

hydrodynamics.title: High-Fidelity Coastal Modeling
hydrodynamics.intro:
Investigating long-term morphological changes and wave-current interactions in complex coastal regions using coupled 3D numerical models.

This project emphasizes the role of localized turbulence and bed shear stress in sediment transport, validating numerical predictions against large-scale laboratory flume experiments.

hydrodynamics.title: Turbulent Flow Structures in Open Channels
hydrodynamics.intro: Large Eddy Simulation (LES) of turbulent structures and secondary flows in rough-bed open channels. Our modeling captures the intricate flow field variations over complex riverbeds.

hydrology.title: Catchment Modeling and Runoff Predictions
hydrology.intro: Developing integrated physics-based models combined with statistical tools to forecast streamflow and assess water availability in ungauged basins.

hydrology.title: Climate Change Impacts on Water Cycles
hydrology.intro: Evaluating the long-term impacts of climate shifts on extreme hydrological events using statistical downscaling of Global Climate Models (GCMs) and regional hydrological routing.

toolsDocs.title: HydroVis-WebGL Toolkit (Open Source Tool)
toolsDocs.intro: A lightweight, browser-based visualizer for massive unstructured mesh hydrodynamic outputs. Supports dynamic rendering, interactive temporal playback, and custom academic colormaps without requiring standalone software like Paraview or Tecplot.

toolsDocs.title: Catchment-ML Toolkit (Open Source Tool)
toolsDocs.intro: A Python-based framework tailored for applying various machine learning models (e.g., LSTM, Random Forest) to ungauged basin runoff predictions and streamflow reconstruction across global catchments.

toolsDocs.title: My First Project Documentation (Note/Article)
toolsDocs.intro: This is an example introduction. You can summarize the core mechanism of your tool, research findings, or methodology here.
